fields.name: No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the involved entry or record, according to the specified number series.

fields.name: Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the bank where you have the bank account.

fields.name: Search Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies an alternate name that you can use to search for the record in question when you cannot remember the value in the Name field.

fields.name: Name 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies additional information for the bank's name. You can enter a maximum of 30 characters, both numbers and letters.

fields.name: Address
fields.tooltip: Specifies the address of the bank where you have the bank account.

fields.name: Address 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies additional address information.

fields.name: City
fields.tooltip: Specifies the city of the bank where you have the bank account.

fields.name: Contact
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the bank employee regularly contacted in connection with this bank account.

fields.name: Phone No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the telephone number of the bank where you have the bank account.

fields.name: Telex No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the telex number of the bank where you have the bank account. You can enter a maximum of 20 characters, both numbers and letters.
Use a standard format to enter the telex number so that it will look uniform on printouts.

fields.name: Bank Account No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number used by the bank for the bank account.

fields.name: Transit No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies a bank identification number of your own choice.

fields.name: Territory Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a territory code for the bank account. To see the territory codes in the Territory table, choose the field.
You can use this field to group bank accounts by territory. After you have entered the codes in the Territory Code field on the individual bank accounts, you can set a filter so that the program will display only bank accounts with the specified territory code.

fields.name: Global Dimension 1 Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a dimension value code for a dimension. To see the dimension value codes you have set up for this dimension in the Dimension Values window, choose the field.
If you enter a dimension value code in this field, the program will use the corresponding dimension value as default each time you enter the bank account on an entry line. Default dimension values are only suggestions and you can change them on the entry line, for example in a journal.
Click here to learn more about dimensions.

fields.name: Global Dimension 2 Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a dimension value code for a dimension. To see the dimension value codes you have set up for this dimension in the Dimension Values window, choose the field.
If you enter a dimension value code in this field, the program will use the corresponding dimension value as default each time you enter the bank account on an entry line. Default dimension values are only suggestions and you can change them on the entry line, for example in a journal.
Click here to learn more about dimensions.

fields.name: Chain Name
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the name of the bank. If you have several accounts at different branches of the same bank, you can use this field to search for all the accounts at that bank.
You can enter a maximum of 20 characters, both numbers and letters.

fields.name: Min. Balance
fields.tooltip: Specifies a minimum balance for the bank account.

fields.name: Bank Acc. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies a code for the bank account posting group for the bank account.

fields.name: Currency Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the currency code for the bank account.

fields.name: Language Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the language that is used when translating specified text on documents to foreign business partner, such as an item description on an order confirmation.

fields.name: Format Region
fields.tooltip: Specifies the region format that is used when formatting specified dates and numbers on documents to foreign business partner, such as an item amount on an order confirmation.

fields.name: Statistics Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies a number to assign to this bank account to a group for statistical purposes.

fields.name: Our Contact Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a code to specify the employee who is responsible for this bank account.

fields.name: Country/Region Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the country/region of the address.

fields.name: Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies an amount, for example rent, that is paid regularly.

fields.name: Comment
fields.tooltip:
Indicates whether a comment has been entered for this bank account.
For example, the comment could describe a particular problem that requires attention when dealing with this bank account.
You can view or create comments in the Comment Sheet window or in the Notes FactBox. For more information, see How to: Create Notes and Notifications.

fields.name: Blocked
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the related record is blocked from being posted in transactions, for example a customer that is declared insolvent or an item that is placed in quarantine.

fields.name: Last Statement No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the last bank account statement that was reconciled with this bank account.

fields.name: Last Payment Statement No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the last bank statement that was imported, either as a feed or a file.

fields.name: Pmt. Rec. No. Series
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number series for payment reconciliation journals.

fields.name: Last Date Modified
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date when the Bank Account card was last modified.

fields.name: Date Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the date interval that values are filtered by.
If this field contains starting and ending dates for a date interval, then other field values only show information for that interval.
You can enter a date interval according to the following rules.


Meaning


Sample expression


Entries that are included


Equal to

12 15 12

Only entries that are posted on December 15, 2012.


Interval

12 15 12..01 15 13
..12 15 12

Entries that are posted on dates between and including December 15, 2012, and January 15, 2013.
Entries that are posted on January 15, 2012, or earlier.


Either/or

12 15 12|12 16 12

Entries that are posted on either December 15, 2012, or December 16, 2012. If there are entries that are posted on both days, then all entries are displayed.


You can also combine the various format types.


Sample expression


Entries that are included


12 15 12|12 01 12..12 10 12

Entries that are posted either on December 15, 2012, or on dates between and including December 1, 2012, and December 10, 2012.


..12 14 12|12 30 12..

Entries that are posted on December 14, 2012, or earlier, or entries that are posted on December 30, 2012, or later, which includes all entries except those that are posted on dates between and including December 15, 2012, and December 29, 2012.

fields.name: Global Dimension 1 Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies if you can set a filter for a dimension so that fields such as Balance (LCY) will display the balance only for entries that relate to dimension values included in the filter.
To see the dimension values you have set up for a dimension in the Dimension Value table, choose the field
Click here to learn more about dimensions.

fields.name: Global Dimension 2 Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies if you can set a filter for a dimension so that fields such as Balance (LCY) will display the balance only for entries that relate to dimension values included in the filter.
To see the dimension values you have set up for a dimension in the Dimension Value table, choose the field
Click here to learn more about dimensions.

fields.name: Balance
fields.tooltip: Specifies the bank account's balance.

fields.name: Balance (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the bank account's current balance in LCY.

fields.name: Net Change
fields.tooltip:
Displays the net value of entries for the bank account, denominated in the applicable foreign currency, for the period entered in the Date Filter field.
The program uses the Amount field in the Bank Account Ledger Entry table (the corresponding LCY amount posted from the general journal) to calculate and update the contents of this field.
You can filter the Net Change field so that its contents are calculated only on the basis of certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values and/or dates.
To see the bank account ledger entries that comprise the amount shown, choose the field.

fields.name: Net Change (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Displays the net value of entries for the bank account, converted to LCY, for the period entered in the Date Filter field.
The program uses the Amount (LCY) field in the Bank Account Ledger Entry table (the corresponding LCY amount posted from the general journal) to calculate and update the contents of this field.
You can filter the Net Change (LCY) field so that its contents are calculated only on the basis of certain Global Dimension 1 values, Global Dimension 2 values and/or dates.
To see the bank account ledger entries that comprise the amount shown, choose the field.

fields.name: Total on Checks
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount of the balance on the bank account that exists on check ledger entries.

fields.name: Use as Default for Currency
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether this is the default company account for payments in sales and service documents in the currency specified for this account. Each currency can have only one default bank account.

fields.name: Fax No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the fax number of the bank where you have the bank account.

fields.name: Telex Answer Back
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the telex answer back code of the bank where you have the bank account. You can enter a maximum of 20 characters, both numbers and letters.
Use a standard format to enter the telex answer back code so that it will look uniform on printouts.

fields.name: Post Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the postal code.

fields.name: County
fields.tooltip: Specifies the state, province or county as a part of the address.

fields.name: Last Check No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the check number of the last check issued from the bank account.

fields.name: Balance Last Statement
fields.tooltip: Specifies the balance amount of the last statement reconciliation on the bank account.

fields.name: Balance at Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the bank account's balance on the last date included in the Date Filter field.

fields.name: Balance at Date (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the bank account's balance in LCY on the last date included in the Date Filter field.

fields.name: Debit Amount
fields.tooltip:
Shows the total of the debit entries that have been posted to the bank account.
To see the ledger entries that comprise the debit amount, choose the field.

fields.name: Credit Amount
fields.tooltip:
Displays the total of the credit entries that have been posted to the bank account.
To see the ledger entries that comprise the credit amount, choose the field.

fields.name: Debit Amount (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Shows the total of the debit entries, in LCY, that have been posted to the bank account.
To see the ledger entries that comprise the debit amount (in LCY), choose the field.

fields.name: Credit Amount (LCY)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the total of the credit entries, in LCY, that have been posted to the bank account.
To see the ledger entries that comprise the credit amount (in LCY), choose the field.

fields.name: Bank Branch No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies a number of the bank branch.

fields.name: E-Mail
fields.tooltip: Specifies the email address associated with the bank account.

fields.name: Home Page
fields.tooltip: Specifies the bank web site.

fields.name: No. Series
fields.tooltip: Contains the code for the number series that was used to assign the bank account's number.

fields.name: Check Report ID
fields.tooltip: Specifies a report ID (an integer) if you want to use a specific report layout. The report ID tells the program which report to use when a check is printed.

fields.name: Check Report Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name that corresponds to the report ID.

fields.name: IBAN
fields.tooltip: Specifies the bank account's international bank account number.

fields.name: SWIFT Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the international bank identifier code (SWIFT) of the bank where you have the account.

fields.name: Bank Statement Import Format
fields.tooltip: Specifies the format of the bank statement file that can be imported into this bank account.

fields.name: Credit Transfer Msg. Nos.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number series for bank instruction messages that are created with the export file that you create from the Direct Debit Collect. Entries window.

fields.name: Direct Debit Msg. Nos.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number series that will be used on the direct debit file that you export for a direct-debit collection entry in the Direct Debit Collect. Entries window.

fields.name: SEPA Direct Debit Exp. Format
fields.tooltip: Specifies the SEPA format of the bank file that will be exported when you choose the Create Direct Debit File button in the Direct Debit Collect. Entries window.

fields.name: Transaction Import Timespan
fields.tooltip: Specifies how far back in time to get new bank transactions for.

fields.name: Automatic Stmt. Import Enabled
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the service is enabled.

fields.name: IntercompanyEnable
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether this bank account is enabled to be copied by IC Partners to make intercompany transactions.

fields.name: Creditor No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies your company as the creditor in connection with payment collection from customers using SEPA Direct Debit.

fields.name: Payment Export Format
fields.tooltip: Specifies the format of the bank file that will be exported when you choose the Export Payments to File button in the Payment Journal window.

fields.name: Bank Clearing Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for bank clearing that is required according to the format standard you selected in the Bank Clearing Standard field.

fields.name: Bank Clearing Standard
fields.tooltip: Specifies the format standard to be used in bank transfers if you use the Bank Clearing Code field to identify you as the sender.

fields.name: Match Tolerance Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies by which tolerance the automatic payment application function will apply the Amount Incl. Tolerance Matched rule for this bank account.

fields.name: Match Tolerance Value
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the automatic payment application function will apply the Amount Incl. Tolerance Matched rule by Percentage or Amount.

fields.name: Disable Automatic Pmt Matching
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether to disable automatic payment matching after importing bank transactions for this bank account.

fields.name: Disable Bank Rec. Optimization
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether to disable bank reconciliation optimization for this bank account. It will result in more precise matches, but it will be slower. Disabling the optimization is useful when there are several bank ledger entries with the same amount and date that need to be automatched.

fields.name: Positive Pay Export Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a code for the data exchange definition that manages the export of positive-pay files.

fields.name: Check Transmitted
fields.tooltip: Specifies to check transmitted before posting the Payment Journal

fields.name: Mobile Phone No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the mobile telephone number of the bank where you have the bank account.
